leagueBriefing: Saturday's Bundesliga action was a masterclass in late-game drama, featuring a combined 23 goals across six matches. While Borussia Dortmund tightened their grip on a Champions League spot with a clinical road win, the rest of the top-four contenders struggled to find separation. Most notably, a six-goal thriller in Freiburg and a dramatic comeback in Leipzig kept fans on the edge of their seats until the final whistles.

games.headline: Ginter's late header denies Leverkusen comeback in six-goal thriller
games.brief: In a match that swung back and forth like a pendulum, SC Freiburg and Bayer Leverkusen shared the points in a chaotic 3-3 draw. Leverkusen looked to have completed a spirited comeback when Martin Terrier put them ahead early in the second half, but a late Matthias Ginter header salvaged a point for the hosts. The result leaves Leverkusen still chasing the pack in a crowded race for the top four.
bullets: Matthias Ginter scored the equalizer in the 86th minute and had a potential winner ruled out for offside moments later.
Alejandro Grimaldo netted his fifth direct free-kick of the season, a high across Europe's top leagues.
Freiburg's Yuito Suzuki and Vincenzo Grifo both found the net in a dominant first half for the hosts.
Christian Kofane and Martin Terrier spearheaded the Leverkusen attack, briefly turning a 2-1 deficit into a 3-2 lead.
Freiburg generated 22 shots compared to Leverkusen's 17 in one of the season's most offensive displays.
games.context: Leverkusen remains two points behind Stuttgart in the battle for the final Champions League spot.

highlights.title: Massive Point At Home Secured! | SC FREIBURG - BAYER 04 LEVERKUSEN | Highlights | MD 25 – Bundesliga

games.headline: Dortmund punishes 10-man Cologne to solidify top-four standing
games.brief: Borussia Dortmund bounced back from their Der Klassiker heartbreak with a gritty, if unspectacular, victory in Cologne. Serhou Guirassy and Maximilian Beier provided the goals, but the match turned on a first-half red card for Cologne's Jahmai Simpson-Pusey. Despite a late scare, Dortmund held on to move eight points clear of the fifth-place spot, all but securing their return to Europe's elite competition.
bullets: Serhou Guirassy scored his 12th goal of the campaign in the 16th minute.
Jahmai Simpson-Pusey was sent off just before halftime after a VAR review upgraded his booking to a red.
Julian Brandt provided a key assist in what was confirmed to be his final season with the club.
Maximilian Beier doubled the lead on the hour mark before Jakub Kaminski pulled one back for the 10-man hosts.
The win marks Dortmund's first league victory since mid-February.
games.context: Dortmund is now eight points clear of fifth-place RB Leipzig in the standings.

highlights.title: Guirassy & Beier Score At Close Win! | 1. FC KÖLN - BORUSSIA DORTMUND | Highlights | Bundesliga

games.headline: Stoppage-time own goal gifts Leipzig dramatic comeback win
games.brief: RB Leipzig avoided a major slip-up at home, needing a stroke of luck in the dying seconds to overcome a resilient Augsburg. After falling behind to a Robin Fellhauer strike, Leipzig huffed and puffed until Yan Diomande leveled the score late. Just as it looked like they would drop points, a scuffed cross from David Raum was turned into his own net by Arthur Chaves, sending the Red Bull Arena into a frenzy.
bullets: Augsburg's Keven Schlotterbeck missed an early penalty before Robin Fellhauer opened the scoring in the 39th minute.
Teenager Yan Diomande scored the equalizer in the 76th minute, continuing his breakout season.
The winner was an own goal by Arthur Chaves in the 90th minute following a David Raum cross.
Leipzig dominated the statistics with 17 shots and 86% pass accuracy.
Willi Orbán was a standout for the hosts, winning a match-high 9.9 rating for his defensive display.
games.context: Leipzig moves level on points with fourth-place Stuttgart in the hunt for Champions League football.

games.headline: Da Costa's stoppage-time header stuns Stuttgart in Mainz
games.brief: VfB Stuttgart's grip on fourth place loosened slightly after a wild 2-2 draw in Mainz. Stuttgart appeared to have the game won after scoring twice in 61 seconds to overturn a halftime deficit, but they couldn't close the door. In the first minute of added time, Danny da Costa reacted quickest to a rebound off the crossbar to head home an equalizer, leaving the visitors frustrated and the home crowd in raptures.
bullets: Danny da Costa scored the equalizer in the 91st minute after Phillipp Mwene hit the woodwork.
Ermedin Demirovic and Deniz Undav scored within a minute of each other (76', 77') to briefly lead the game.
Lee Jae-sung opened the scoring for Mainz in the 39th minute with a close-range header.
Stuttgart dominated the early stages, forcing goalkeeper Daniel Batz into several high-difficulty saves.
The draw prevents Stuttgart from pulling away in the race for the top four.
games.context: Stuttgart is now level on points with fifth-place RB Leipzig with nine games remaining.

games.headline: Prass brace keeps Hoffenheim's Champions League dream alive
games.brief: Hoffenheim took a significant step toward a historic Champions League berth by dispatching last-placed Heidenheim in a clinical 4-2 display. Alexander Prass was the star of the show, bagging two goals before the break to put the visitors in total control. While Heidenheim's Luca Kerber fought back with a brace of his own, the gap in quality was evident as Hoffenheim stayed firmly in third place.
bullets: Alexander Prass scored twice in the first half (26', 45') to set the tone for Hoffenheim.
Fisnik Asllani and Tim Lemperle added second-half goals to put the match out of reach.
Luca Kerber provided the silver lining for Heidenheim with a two-goal performance.
Hoffenheim outshot the hosts 16-8 and maintained 54% of the possession.
The win keeps Hoffenheim in pole position for a top-three finish this season.
games.context: Hoffenheim remains in third place, strengthening their bid for European football next season.

games.headline: Hamburg penalty clinicality pushes Wolfsburg closer to the brink
games.brief: In a match dominated by the referee's whistle, Hamburger SV came from behind to pile the misery on a sinking Wolfsburg. All three goals came from the penalty spot, with Hamburg remaining perfect from 12 yards to secure a vital road win. The defeat sparked angry protests at the Volkswagen Arena, as fans threw flaming scarves onto the pitch to voice their frustration at the club's impending relegation battle.
bullets: Every goal in the match was scored from a penalty kick.
Christian Eriksen gave Wolfsburg an early lead before Luka Vušković and Jean-Luc Dompé converted for Hamburg.
The match was interrupted by fan protests as Wolfsburg fell into the automatic relegation zone.
Wolfsburg has now gone eight games without a victory.
Hamburg moves into a comfortable mid-table position with 29 points.
games.context: Wolfsburg drops into 17th place, facing their first relegation since 1997.
